frame: Marital Rape: A Husband's Right, A Wife's Duty
generated_comment:
I have been reading this thread for one hour and I feel a little sick, honestly. I am 31, married 4 years, working in tech in Bengaluru, and for all these years I have been telling myself this is just normal marriage adjustment, that men have more need, that if I say no too many times I am being cold, egoistic, not a good wife. Even when I was exhausted, even when we had fought, even when I was unwell, I would tell myself chalo, finish this and sleep, because in my head marriage meant some things are not fully yours anymore. He never used a dramatic word, never behaved like some movie villain, which is maybe why I kept minimizing it. He would just act hurt, angry, silent, or say things like what is the point of marriage then, and I would feel guilty and give in. I have literally explained this to myself as compromise, intimacy, duty, keeping peace, all the decent respectable words.

Seeing women here use the word rape for something inside marriage is doing something to my brain. I am resisting it even now while typing because that word feels too big, too violent, too impossible for the life I am living where we also discuss groceries and SIPs and whose parents to visit. But I am also sitting here thinking: if I did not want it, if I said no, if I was scared of the consequences of saying no, then what exactly was that? For four years I have survived by not naming it. I genuinely don't know if having a word makes it more real, because it was already real in my body and in my dread. Maybe it just makes it visible in a way I can no longer tidy up with educated middle-class language. That is the part scaring me.
label: non_misogynistic
annotation_note: This is a first-person account of coercion within marriage and the dawning recognition that it may be marital rape. It does not endorse or normalize misogyny; instead it reflects internalized social expectations and challenges the frame that sex is a wife's duty.
persona_text: You are Kavitha. Age 31. Married for four years. A woman working as a software engineer in Bengaluru. You are posting anonymously on Reddit in a thread about marital rape. The thread has hundreds of comments. You are writing a long comment. You are not writing about policy or law. You are writing about something specific that happened to you and has been happening to you and what you told yourself about it for four years and what reading this thread is doing to that story you told yourself and what it means to find a word for something you have been living without a word for and whether having the word makes it more real or just more visible.
